paper_title: Anti-tumor efficacy of a potent and selective non-covalent KRAS G12D inhibitor

stages.name: Structure Prediction
stages.objective: Validate KRAS G12D protein fold and identify flexible regions
paper_claims: KRAS G12D adopts canonical G-domain fold
Switch I (30-40) and Switch II (58-75) are conformationally flexible
P-loop (10-17) contains well-defined G12D mutation site

stages.name: Drug Binding Pose
stages.objective: Determine if MRTX1133 binds the Switch II pocket as claimed
paper_claims: MRTX1133 binds in the Switch II pocket
Binding induces conformational changes in Switch I and Switch II
Drug contacts residues in the Switch II region (58-75)

stages.name: Binding Affinity & Complex Structure
stages.objective: Estimate binding affinity and validate protein-ligand complex
paper_claims: MRTX1133 has IC50 < 2 nM against KRAS G12D
SPR KD ~0.2 pM for GDP-bound KRAS G12D

stages.name: Conformational Dynamics
stages.objective: Simulate KRAS G12D with and without MRTX1133 to validate conformational trapping mechanism
paper_claims: MRTX1133 traps Switch I/II in effector-incompatible conformation
Conformational trapping occurs in both GDP and GTP-bound states
Drug binding blocks RAF/SOS effector interaction surfaces

stages.name: Effector Disruption
stages.objective: Test if MRTX1133-bound KRAS G12D blocks RAF RBD binding
paper_claims: MRTX1133 blocks RAF RBD interaction with IC50 of 9 nM
Drug-bound conformation is incompatible with effector binding

expected_outcome: If the paper's claims are valid, we expect: (1) AF/ESMFold confirm the G-domain fold, (2) DiffDock/GNINA place MRTX1133 in the Switch II pocket matching PDB 7T47, (3) Boltz-2/Vina estimate binding affinity in the low-nM range for G12D and much weaker for WT, (4) GROMACS shows Switch II rigidification upon drug binding, (5) PRODIGY shows reduced KRAS-RAF binding energy in the drug-bound state.
